revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:足長神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ashinaga Shrine |image = File:Asinaga jinja haiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.025228|N|138.133883|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi|qid=Q109554668|1=Ashinazuchi}} |status = {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=Sub-shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine |type = {{ill|Mura Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} (Former status) |founded = Unknown |main_building= [[Nagare-zukuri]] |other_name = Ogimiya (荻宮) |annual_festival = November 27 |prefecture= {{ill|Suwa|qid=Q846338|1=Suwa, Nagano}} }} '''Ashinaga Shrine''' (足長神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Shiga|ja|四賀村 (長野県諏訪郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga|1=Shiga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17176077}} area of {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is a branch shrine (末社) of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. The shrine's historical designation under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was as a {{ill|Mura Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine was formerly called Ogimiya (荻宮) due to its thatched reed roof. It is situated on the slopes of the {{ill|Kuwabara Castle|en|Kuwabara Castle|qid=Q2969506|lt=Kuwahara Castle|1=Kuwabara Castle}} ruins. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|qid=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi no Mikoto|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}''' (also known as Ashinaga Hikokami). ** This deity is a retainer of the Suwa Taisha deity, [[Takeminakata]]. ** In Japanese mythology, Ashinazuchi appears as the father of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, an ancestor of Takeminakata. The nearby {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} enshrines Tenazuchi, Ashinazuchi's consort. == History == According to the ''Suwa Historical Records''<ref>Reprint edition: Suwa Historical Society, ''Suwa Historical Records''. Gendosha, 1996. {{Cite book|NCID=BN15058010|qq=}}</ref>, the two deities Ashinaga and Tenaga were originally worshiped together when the Kuwahara region was united as one community. Later, the shrines were separated into the upper and lower Kuwahara regions, with Ashinaga enshrined in the upper and Tenaga in the lower. However, the area is named "Ashinaga-yama" (足長山), and historical documents suggest the deities were introduced to match existing place names, casting doubt on whether the separation was a deliberate act of syncretism or coincidental. In the {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daido era|1=Daidō}} (806–810 CE), Mikoto Arikazu, an ancestor of the chief priest (大祝) of Suwa Taisha, built a large shrine here out of reverence for Ashinaga. The shrine was officially listed as a branch of Suwa Taisha. Documents from the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592) and the {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpo era|1=Tenpō}} (1830–1844) record subsequent renovations. == Cultural Properties == The shrine has several structures designated as cultural properties by Suwa City: * Haiden and Maiya (Tangible Cultural Property) - Designated in 1988. * Honden (Tangible Cultural Property) - Designated in 1994. == location == * Address: {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Shiga Ashinagayama 5386, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} {{ill|Kami-Suwa Station|en|Kami-Suwa Station|qid=Q844049|lt=Kami-Suwa Station|1=Kami-Suwa Station}} (approximately 40 minutes on foot). == References == * ''Nagano Prefecture Place names'', {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}. * {{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken'ichi Tanigawa|qq=}} (Editor), ''The Gods of Japan, Volume 9: Mino, Hida, and Shinano'', Hakusensha, 2000. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Links == * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} (Suwa City) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|脚摩乳・手摩乳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://f3-list.net/jinja/737.html Gendōjin name Registry: Ashinaga Shrine] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Suwa Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|足長神社|version=92473065|comment=Imported full ja history}}
